question_text: Whether or not you have ever voted or intend to vote in the future, whom would you like to win the 2027 presidential election?
source_title: TIFA National Poll 2026: Political Alignments and 2027 Election Prospects
notes: Extracted from TIFA grouped chart wave: August 2025 | Automated extraction failed (source fetch could not resolve DNS at build time; see data/sources_registry.json and data/review_queue.json for the failed attempt). Figures were entered manually and cross-checked against public news coverage of this release. | Sample size and fieldwork dates confirmed via public reporting (Capital FM, 11 Sept 2025): n=2,023 across all 47 counties.

question_text: Whether or not you have ever voted or intend to vote in the future, whom would you like to win the 2027 presidential election?
source_title: TIFA National Poll 2026: Political Alignments and 2027 Election Prospects
notes: Extracted from TIFA grouped chart wave: November 2025 | Automated extraction failed (source fetch could not resolve DNS at build time; see data/sources_registry.json and data/review_queue.json for the failed attempt). Figures were entered manually and cross-checked against public news coverage of this release. | Sample size and fieldwork dates confirmed via public reporting (Nairobi Wire, 19 Dec 2025): n=2,053, margin of error ±2.16%.

source_title: Infotrak National Survey: 2027 Presidential Candidates' Popularity (December 2025)
notes: Manually researched and cross-checked against five independent published reports (Daily Nation, The Standard, Tuko, Kenyans.co.ke, Nairobi Wire) of Infotrak's December 2025 national survey; figures consistent across all sources. Fieldwork 19-20 December 2025, n=1,000, CATI (Computer-Assisted Telephone Interviews) methodology, nationally representative. Automated scraping was not attempted for this record; added via manual research specifically to diversify pollster coverage beyond TIFA. Exact question wording not confirmed from available sources, so left blank rather than assumed.

source_title: Ruto Takes Early Lead In 2027 Race As Kalonzo Comes Distant Third
notes: Manually researched and cross-checked against a second independent write-up (OmarosaOmarosa, 29 Apr 2026) reporting the same Stats Kenya survey with matching figures at coarser rounding (23.4%, 12.3%, 8.2%, 7.4%, 4%). Methodology: telephone interviews, conducted March 2026. Exact fieldwork dates and sample size were not confirmed from available sources, so left blank rather than assumed. Automated scraping was not attempted for this record; added via manual research to further diversify pollster and methodology coverage (telephone, distinct from TIFA's face-to-face and Infotrak's CATI).

question_text: Whether or not you have ever voted or intend to vote in the future, whom would you like to win the 2027 presidential election?
source_title: TIFA National Poll 2026: 1st Release on Political Alignments and 2027 Election Prospects
notes: Extracted from TIFA grouped chart wave: May 2026 | Automated extraction failed at first attempt (DNS resolution error in the original build sandbox); figures were entered manually and cross-checked against public news coverage of this release. | Sample size and question text confirmed via public reporting of the May 2026 release (margin of error ±2.18%, fieldwork May 2–11 2026, n=2,013 across 47 counties). | CORRECTED 2026-07-08: a subsequent automated GitHub Actions run reached the real PDF (real internet access on the runner, unlike the original sandbox) but its chart-parsing logic misread the values (produced Ruto=2.0, Kalonzo=3.0, Matiang'i=14.0, Gachagua=9.0, Sifuna=10.0 at a false 0.98 confidence) and silently overwrote this manually-verified record, because the dedup logic ranked records by raw confidence number and treated this record's null confidence (a human verified it directly, so there is no extractor confidence to report) as 0, lower than the wrong automated extraction's 0.98. That dedup bug is fixed in backend/poll_tracker.py (manual verification now always outranks automated extraction). Figures restored to the correct, manually-verified values.
